problems.title: Jump Game Reachability
problems.prompt: You are given an integer array nums. You are initially positioned at the first index, and each element represents your maximum jump length at that position. Return true if you can reach the last index, otherwise false.

problems.title: Gas Station Problem
problems.prompt: There are n gas stations along a circular route. gas[i] is the amount of gas at station i, and cost[i] is the cost to travel from i to the next station. Return the starting station index if you can travel around the circuit once in the clockwise direction, otherwise return -1. If a solution exists, it is unique.

problems.title: Merge Intervals
problems.prompt: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.

problems.title: Non-overlapping Intervals
problems.prompt: Given an array of intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], return the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.

problems.title: Candy (Lite)
problems.prompt: There are n children standing in a line. Each child is assigned a rating value given in ratings. You give candies following: each child gets at least one candy, and children with a higher rating get more candies than their neighbors. Return the minimum number of candies you need.

problems.title: Task Scheduler Assign
problems.prompt: You are given a char array tasks representing CPU tasks, and a non-negative integer n representing the cooldown between two same tasks. Each unit of time, the CPU can complete one task or be idle. Return the least number of units of time the CPU will take to finish all given tasks.
constraints: tasks[i] is an uppercase English letter

problems.title: Jump Game II
problems.prompt: You are given a 0-indexed array of integers nums of length n. You are initially positioned at nums[0]. Each element nums[i] represents the maximum length of a forward jump from index i. Return the minimum number of jumps to reach nums[n - 1]. The test cases are generated so that you can reach nums[n - 1].

problems.title: Minimum Number of Arrows
problems.prompt: There are some spherical balloons taped onto a flat wall represented by points. points[i] = [xstart, xend] describes a balloon whose horizontal diameter stretches between xstart and xend. You shoot an arrow at x and burst all balloons where xstart <= x <= xend. Return the minimum number of arrows that must be shot to burst all balloons.
